The gospel announces freedom to those burdened by fears. Let those who are tired and weighed down under the burden of sin find relief in Christ, shake themselves free from the dust of their doubts and fears, and break loose from those bonds. The price paid by the Redeemer for our salvation was not silver or gold, or anything perishable, but his own precious blood. Considering the freeness of this salvation, and how harmful sins are to earthly comfort, we will value more the redemption that is in Christ. Do we seek victory over every sin, remembering that God’s glory requires holiness in every follower of Christ? The good news is that the Lord Jesus reigns. Christ himself first brought these tidings. His ministers proclaim these good tidings, keeping themselves clean from the pollutions of the world; they are beautiful to those to whom they are sent. Zion's watchmen could scarcely see any sign of God’s favor through the dark cloud of their afflictions, but now the cloud is scattered, they will plainly see the fulfillment. Zion's desolate places will then rejoice; all the world will benefit from this. This applies to our salvation by Christ. Babylon is no place for Israelites. And it is a call to all who are in bondage to sin and Satan, to use the freedom that Christ has proclaimed. They were to go with diligent haste, not to lose time or linger, but they were not to go with distrustful haste. Those who are on the path of duty are under God’s special protection; and whoever believes this will not hurry out of fear.

Here begins that wonderful, detailed, and faithful description of the office, character, and glory of the Messiah, which has convinced many of the most hardened unbelievers. Christ is Wisdom itself; in the work of our redemption there appeared the wisdom of God in a mystery. Those who saw him said, Surely no one ever looked so miserable; never was sorrow like unto his sorrow. But God greatly exalted him. That will be revealed by the gospel of Christ, which could never be told in any other way. And Christ having once shed his blood for sinners, its power continues. May all opponents see the wisdom of ceasing from their opposition, and be made partakers of the blood of sprinkling, and the baptism of the Holy Ghost; obeying him, and praising his salvation.
